properties.displayName: Check Point Email Security (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorUiConfig.title: Check Point Email Security (via Codeless Connector Framework)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown: The [Check Point Email Security (Harmony Email Collaboration)](https://www.checkpoint.com/harmony/email-security/) data connector provides the capability to ingest security events and audit logs from Check Point's Email Security platform into Microsoft Sentinel through the REST API. The connector provides visibility into advanced email threats including zero-day threats, phishing, account takeover, data leakage, and shadow IT discovery. It ingests security events, anti-phishing exceptions, spam exceptions, and audit logs into Microsoft Sentinel, helping organizations maintain security and compliance visibility.

sampleQueries.description: Get sample of Check Point Email Security Events
Get sample of Check Point Anti-Phishing Exceptions
Get sample of Check Point Spam Exceptions
Get sample of Check Point Audit Logs

dataTypes.name: CheckPointEmailSecurityEvents_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CheckPointEmailSecurityEvents_CL
| where ingestion_time() > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(ingestion_time())
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CheckPointEmailSecAntiPhishingExceptions_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CheckPointEmailSecAntiPhishingExceptions_CL
| where ingestion_time() > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(ingestion_time())
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CheckPointEmailSecuritySpamExceptions_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CheckPointEmailSecuritySpamExceptions_CL
| where ingestion_time() > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(ingestion_time())
| where isnotempty(Time)

dataTypes.name: CheckPointEmailSecurityAuditLogs_CL
dataTypes.lastDataReceivedQuery:
CheckPointEmailSecurityAuditLogs_CL
| where ingestion_time() > ago(12h)
| summarize Time = max(ingestion_time())
| where isnotempty(Time)

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: Read and Write permissions are required.

customs.name: Check Point Email Security API Credentials
customs.description: Two separate Check Point Infinity Portal API keys are required. A **Harmony Email & Collaboration** API key (**Client ID** + **Client Secret**) is used for security events and exceptions. A separate **Logs as a Service** API key (**Audit Client ID** + **Audit Client Secret**) is required for audit logs. Generate both from your Check Point Infinity Portal under Global Settings > API Keys.

instructionSteps.title: Connect Check Point Email Security to Microsoft Sentinel

parameters.content: To gather data from Check Point Email Security, you need to provide the following credentials:
**1. API Base URL** - The base URL for your Check Point Email Security tenant (region-specific).
**2. Client ID** - Client ID of the Harmony Email & Collaboration API key (used for security events and exceptions).
**3. Client Secret** - Secret (access key) of the Harmony Email & Collaboration API key.
**4. Audit Client ID** - Client ID of a separate **Logs as a Service** API key (used for audit logs).
**5. Audit Client Secret** - Secret (access key) of the Logs as a Service API key.

To obtain these credentials, log in to your Check Point Infinity Portal and navigate to the **API Keys** section under **Global Settings**. Create one API key with the **Harmony Email & Collaboration** service for the Client ID/Secret, and a second API key with the **Logs as a Service** service for the Audit Client ID/Secret.

> **Multi-tenant support:** This connector supports ingesting data from multiple Check Point Email Security tenants in parallel. Click **Add Connection** once per tenant, supplying that tenant's API Base URL and credentials - each connection is tracked and managed independently in the grid below.

parameters.label: Add Connection
parameters.title: Configure Check Point Email Security API Connection
parameters.subtitle: Connect to Check Point Email Security to ingest security data

parameters.label: Audit Client ID
parameters.placeholder: Your Logs as a Service Client ID
parameters.name: auditClientId

parameters.label: Audit Client Secret
parameters.placeholder: Your Logs as a Service Client Secret
parameters.name: auditClientSecret

schema.name: CheckPointEmailSecurityAuditLogs_CL
schema.description: The CheckPointEmailSecurityAuditLogs table contains audit logs tracking administrative actions and system events from Check Point Email Security that have been ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the log entry was generated.

columns.name: AuditId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the audit log entry.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Severity level of the audit event (e.g., Notice, Warning).

columns.name: CreatedAt
columns.description: Timestamp when the audit log was created.

columns.name: CreatedBy
columns.description: User email who performed the action.

columns.name: Module
columns.description: Module or service that generated the log (e.g., CLOUDINFRA).

columns.name: Category
columns.description: Category of the audit event (e.g., Authentication, Configuration).

columns.name: Event
columns.description: Event type (e.g., Login, Logout, ConfigChange).

columns.name: EventData
columns.description: Additional event data and details.

schema.name: CheckPointEmailSecAntiPhishingExceptions_CL
schema.description: The CheckPointEmailSecAntiPhishingExceptions table contains anti-phishing whitelist and exception rules from Check Point Email Security that have been ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the log entry was generated.

columns.name: EntityId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the exception.

columns.name: AttachmentMd5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the attachment.

columns.name: SenderEmail
columns.description: Email address of the sender.

columns.name: SenderName
columns.description: Name of the sender.

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description: Recipient email address.

columns.name: SenderClientIp
columns.description: Client IP address of the sender.

columns.name: SenderDomain
columns.description: Domain of the sender.

columns.name: SenderIp
columns.description: IP address of the sender.

columns.name: LinkDomains
columns.description: Domains contained in email links.

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: Email subject line.

columns.name: Comment
columns.description: Comment associated with the exception.

columns.name: ActionNeeded
columns.description: Action required for the exception.

columns.name: MatchOnlyFuture
columns.description: Indicates if the exception applies only to future emails.

columns.name: QuarantineAll
columns.description: Indicates if all matching emails should be quarantined.

columns.name: IgnoringSpfCheck
columns.description: Indicates if SPF check is ignored.

columns.name: SubjectMatching
columns.description: Subject matching criteria (e.g., contains, equals).

columns.name: LinkDomainMatching
columns.description: Link domain matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderNameMatching
columns.description: Sender name matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderDomainMatching
columns.description: Sender domain matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderEmailMatching
columns.description: Sender email matching criteria.

columns.name: RecipientMatching
columns.description: Recipient matching criteria.

columns.name: AddedBy
columns.description: User ID who added the exception.

columns.name: EditedBy
columns.description: User ID who last edited the exception.

columns.name: UpdateTime
columns.description: Timestamp when the exception was last updated.

schema.name: CheckPointEmailSecurityEvents_CL
schema.description: The CheckPointEmailSecurityEvents table contains security events from Check Point Email Security including phishing, malware, DLP, and other threats that have been ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the log entry was generated.

columns.name: EventId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the security event.

columns.name: CustomerId
columns.description: Customer identifier in the Check Point platform.

columns.name: Saas
columns.description: SaaS platform source (e.g., office365_emails, gmail).

columns.name: EntityId
columns.description: Entity identifier associated with the event.

columns.name: State
columns.description: Current state of the event (e.g., dismissed, active).

columns.name: EventType
columns.description: Type of security event (e.g., dlp, phishing, malware).

columns.name: ConfidenceIndicator
columns.description: Confidence level of the threat detection (e.g., malicious, suspicious).

columns.name: EventCreated
columns.description: Timestamp when the event was created.

columns.name: Severity
columns.description: Severity level of the event (Low, Medium, High, Highest).

columns.name: Data
columns.description: Additional event data.

columns.name: Description
columns.description: Detailed description of the security event.

columns.name: SenderAddress
columns.description: Email address of the sender associated with the event.

columns.name: EntityLink
columns.description: Deep link to the entity in the Check Point portal.

columns.name: Actions
columns.description: Actions taken on or available for the event.

columns.name: AdditionalData
columns.description: Additional parameters and links related to the event.

columns.name: AvailableEventActions
columns.description: List of available actions that can be performed on the event.

schema.name: CheckPointEmailSecuritySpamExceptions_CL
schema.description: The CheckPointEmailSecuritySpamExceptions table contains spam whitelist and exception rules from Check Point Email Security that have been ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

columns.name: TimeGenerated
columns.description: The timestamp (in UTC) when the log entry was generated.

columns.name: EntityId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the spam exception.

columns.name: AttachmentMd5
columns.description: MD5 hash of the attachment.

columns.name: SenderEmail
columns.description: Email address of the sender.

columns.name: SenderName
columns.description: Name of the sender.

columns.name: Recipient
columns.description: Recipient email address.

columns.name: SenderClientIp
columns.description: Client IP address of the sender.

columns.name: SenderDomain
columns.description: Domain of the sender.

columns.name: SenderIp
columns.description: IP address of the sender.

columns.name: LinkDomains
columns.description: Domains contained in email links.

columns.name: Subject
columns.description: Email subject line.

columns.name: Comment
columns.description: Comment associated with the exception.

columns.name: ActionNeeded
columns.description: Action required for the exception.

columns.name: MatchOnlyFuture
columns.description: Indicates if the exception applies only to future emails.

columns.name: QuarantineAll
columns.description: Indicates if all matching emails should be quarantined.

columns.name: IgnoringSpfCheck
columns.description: Indicates if SPF check is ignored.

columns.name: SubjectMatching
columns.description: Subject matching criteria (e.g., contains, equals).

columns.name: LinkDomainMatching
columns.description: Link domain matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderNameMatching
columns.description: Sender name matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderDomainMatching
columns.description: Sender domain matching criteria.

columns.name: SenderEmailMatching
columns.description: Sender email matching criteria.

columns.name: RecipientMatching
columns.description: Recipient matching criteria.

columns.name: AddedBy
columns.description: User ID who added the exception.

columns.name: EditedBy
columns.description: User ID who last edited the exception.

columns.name: UpdateTime
columns.description: Timestamp when the exception was last updated.

properties.displayName: Check Point Email Security (via Codeless Connector Framework)

connectorDefinitionName.defaultValue: Check Point Email Security (via Codeless Connector Framework)

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Check Point Email Security (Harmony Email & Collaboration) Solution for Microsoft Sentinel enables you to ingest security events, anti-phishing exceptions, spam exceptions, and audit logs from Check Point's Email Security platform into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Framework (CCF). This solution provides visibility into advanced email threats including zero-day threats, phishing, account takeover, data leakage, and shadow IT discovery.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

This solution takes a dependency on the following technologies, and some of these dependencies either may be in Preview state or might result in additional ingestion or operational costs:

  1. Codeless Connector Platform (CCP)

Data Connectors: 1

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
